title: Easy Banana & Blueberry Pancakes
summary: Fluffy banana pancakes studded with juicy blueberries, ready in minutes. Perfect for the little ones - and grown-ups too.

method.description:
1 Adult Serving = 2 Kids Servings
Defrost your frozen blueberries for the pancakes (you can skip this step if you're using fresh ones).
If you're up for the quick compote, tip the frozen blueberries into the pan with the water and honey and bring to a boil. Simmer for a minute then remove from the heat and allow to cool.

To make the pancakes, add the ripe banana to a bowl and mash together until smooth. Crack the egg into the bowl and whisk everything together. Add in the self-raising flour and a tiny pinch of salt and mix everything together until well combined, adding a splash of milk if its a little thick.

Melt a little knob of butter in a large frying pan over a medium heat. Scoop in heaped tablespoon of the mixture into the pan, keeping 2-3cm of space around each one, then drop some of the defrosted blueberries onto the surface of the batter. Cook for 2 minutes on the first side, until lightly golden, then flip and cook for another minute. 
Repeat until you've used up all your mix.
Plate up the blueberry pancakes, with your quite compote and yoghurt, or your kid's favourite topping.

tips: These pancakes freeze well — cool fully then freeze with baking paper between each one.
You can skip the compote and just use a drizzle of maple syrup.

chefs.name: Adam Bush
chefs.summary: Adam is a chef, baker and food writer with a love of big, bold flavours. He trained in Michelin-starred kitchens, pubs and a bakery before turning his hand to recipe writing.
